sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE didn't complete the level. The border now has 46 B's and 18 W's (was 47 B's and 17 W's). So SPACE counted as a meaningful action.
Let me think about this differently. The border is counting meaningful actions. The initial state had 64 B's and 0 W's. The current state has 46 B's and 18 W's. So 18 meaningful actions have been performed.
If the goal is to reduce B to 0, I need 46 more meaningful actions. But that seems like a lot for a single level.
Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the goal is not related to the border at all. Maybe the goal is to position the blue squares at specific locations, and the level completes when the blue squares are in the correct positions.
Looking at the face structure, the black shape has two "eye socket" regions at the top. The blue squares are "eyes" that can move within the black shape. The goal might be to move the blue squares to the eye socket positions.
But the blue squares can't reach the eye sockets because of the barrier. So maybe the goal is something else.
Actually, let me look at the face structure more carefully. The black shape has a specific layout. The blue squares are inside the black shape. The face is split vertically into yellow (left) and orange (right) halves.
Maybe the goal is to make the blue squares touch the colored regions (yellow and orange). The left blue square is adjacent to the yellow background, and the right blue square is adjacent to the orange background. This is already the case.
Hmm, let me try a different approach. Let me look at the initial state and see if there's a pattern.
Actually, I just realized something. The initial state had the blue squares at rows 49-53, which is the bottom of the black shape. The current state has the blue squares at rows 54-58, which is even lower.
Wait, but the black shape is at rows 9-58. So rows 54-58 is the bottom of the black shape. The blue squares are now at the bottom of the black shape.
Maybe the goal is to move the blue squares to the bottom of the black shape, which they are now. But the level didn't complete after pressing SPACE.
Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it completes the level.
Actually, that doesn't make sense. SPACE just counts as a meaningful action, and it doesn't complete the level.
Let me try a different approach. Let me look at the face structure and see if there are any other interactive elements.
Actually, I just realized that I haven't tried clicking on the black face shape itself. Maybe clicking on the black face shape does something.
Let me try clicking on the black face shape (not on the blue squares or the eye sockets).

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current border state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Top row: {ascii_lines[0].count('B')} B's, {ascii_lines[0].count('W')} W's")
print(f"Bottom row: {ascii_lines[63].count('B')} B's, {ascii_lines[63].count('W')} W's")

# Let me try clicking on the black face shape (not on blue squares or eye sockets)
# The black face shape is at (9,9)-(58,53). Let me click on a black cell within the face.
# Looking at the ascii, the black face shape has a specific layout.
# Let me click on the black region at row 10, col 10 (top-left of the face)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 10}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
